name: Villanueva del Fresno and Higuera de Vargas Loop
name_original: Higuera de Vargas a Acueducto del Convento de Nuestra Señora de la Luz — circular desde Villanueva del Fresno
description: This moderate 36.1-mile mountain biking route from Villanueva del Fresno explores Extremadura's forests and the historic Aqueduct of Nuestra
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Villanueva Del Fresno traverse a landscape characterized by extensive dehesas, traditional Mediterranean pastures dotted with oak trees. The region features the wilder terrain of the Alcarrache River, with its deep gorges and Mediterranean forest. Additionally, the municipality borders the Great Alqueva Lake, offering routes along its shoreline. This diverse environment provides varied terrain for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Villanueva Del Fresno The most popular mountain bike trail is Dehesas de Alconchel Trail, a difficult 36.5 miles (58.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 6 minutes to complete. This route explores the dehesas landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Communal Lands Trail, a moderate 27.8 miles (44.7 km) path. This route offers a ride through varied communal lands. Local mountain bikers also love the Embalse de Alqueva y Playa de Cheles – ida y vuelta desde Alconchel (Ruta 3 Centro BTT Alqueva), a 33.9 miles (54.6 km) trail leading through the Alqueva reservoir area, often completed in about 4 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Villanueva Del Fresno is defined by dehesas, the Alcarrache River, and the Great Alqueva Lake. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Villanueva Del Fresno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 10 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva Del Fresno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Villanueva Del Fresno?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails around Villanueva Del Fresno, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find 1 easy route, 9 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Villanueva Del Fresno?
A: The terrain in Villanueva Del Fresno is diverse, characterized by extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; (traditional Mediterranean pastures dotted with oak trees), the wilder landscapes of the Alcarrache River with its deep gorges and Mediterranean forest, and routes along the shoreline of the Great Alqueva Lake. This variety provides a scenic backdrop for your rides.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Villanueva Del Fresno?
A: Yes, there is one easy mountain bike trail available, which is suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed ride. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, the easy option provides a good starting point.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails traverse the unique &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; landscape and offer views of the Alcarrache River. The Great Alqueva Lake, the largest artificial lake in Western Europe, is a prominent feature, with routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031546272/embalse-de-alqueva-y-playa-de-cheles-ida-y-vuelta-desde-alconchel-ruta-3-centro-btt-alqueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alqueva y Playa de Cheles&lt;/a&gt; leading through its reservoir area. The region is also known for its rich biodiversity, including various bird species and wildcats.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Villanueva Del Fresno?
A: The mountain bike trails in Villanueva Del Fresno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 5 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain and the natural beauty of the dehesas and lake areas.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, there are circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356974919/cerro-de-lucas-a-acueducto-del-convento-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-luz-circular-por-el-puente-de-la-bogana-desde-villanueva-del-fresno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Lucas a Acueducto del Convento de Nuestra Señora de la Luz&lt;/a&gt; route offers a circular experience, allowing you to start and end in the same location.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villanueva Del Fresno?
A: Villanueva Del Fresno is located in the Extremadura region, which experiences hot summers. The best time for mountain biking is typically during the cooler months of spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are more moderate and the natural landscapes are vibrant.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions to explore near the trails?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, Villanueva del Fresno offers historical attractions. You can find ruins of a 15th-century fortress, the aqueduct of the Convent of Nuestra Señora de la Luz, and various hermitages. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356974919/cerro-de-lucas-a-acueducto-del-convento-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-luz-circular-por-el-puente-de-la-bogana-desde-villanueva-del-fresno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Lucas a Acueducto del Convento de Nuestra Señora de la Luz&lt;/a&gt;, may pass by such landmarks.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in the area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031578039/dehesas-de-alconchel-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesas de Alconchel Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 58.8 km route that explores the dehesas landscape, typically taking over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Can I combine mountain biking with other outdoor activities in Villanueva Del Fresno?
A: Absolutely. The natural environment of Villanueva Del Fresno is ideal for various outdoor pursuits. You can combine mountain biking with hiking, fishing in the Cuncos reservoir, Alcarrache river, or Alqueva lake, and even stargazing in the Alqueva area, which is recognized as a Starlight Destination.

Q: Are there any specific trails that highlight the dehesas landscape?
A: Many routes traverse the dehesas, which are a defining feature of the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351347897/alqueva-btt-center-route-7-dehesas-de-villanueva-del-fresno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alqueva BTT Center - Route 7: Dehesas de Villanueva del Fresno&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase this unique landscape, offering a moderate 41 km ride.

Q: What are some nearby attractions I can visit after a ride?
A: After your ride, you can explore several nearby attractions. Consider visiting the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/612540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monsaraz Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6260983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monsaraz Historic Village&lt;/a&gt;. The ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3909927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cromlech of Xerez&lt;/a&gt; is also an interesting man-made monument to see.
tips: A town full of architectural beauty in a privileged environment that is worth knowing.
Hidden between roads and extensive Extremaduran meadows, we find the beautiful Bogaña bridge that allows us to cross the Alcarrache river.

This small town of almost 2,000 inhabitants has a long history and has numerous places of interest such as El Castillo del Coso, La Ermita Santuario de Nuestra Señora de Loreto and La casa de la Calle del Espíritu Santo.

In a spectacular setting, we come across this Franciscan convent, almost 400 years old (15th century). It is in a state of ruin. Next to it, we can see the aqueduct (18th century), which served to supply water to the building. A place to visit, preferably in spring or autumn.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain biking route is approximately 58 kilometers long. It typically takes around 4 hours and 55 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as having a moderate technical skill difficulty and is considered a moderate mountain bike trip level. It's best suited for riders with some experience.

faqs.question: Where does this circular mountain biking route start and end?
faqs.answer: The route is circular, starting and ending in Villanueva del Fresno. You'll pass through Higuera de Vargas along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The trail is situated in the Extremadura region, characterized by Mediterranean forests, including chestnut, oak, and holm-oak trees. You can expect a mix of natural paths and dirt tracks throughout the ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: While specific recommendations for this trail aren't provided, the Extremadura region generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking in spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) to avoid the summer heat.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key attractions include the town of Plaza de España, Higuera de Vargas and the historic Convent and Aqueduct of Nuestra Señora de la Luz. You'll also pass through the Villanueva del Fresno Town Center and by Puente de la Bogaña.

faqs.question: Does this mountain biking route connect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official paths. Approximately 25% of the trail follows the Camino Natural del Guadiana, and a small portion (1%) is part of the Camino Villanueva.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for this specific route is not available. It's always best to check local regulations or signage regarding dogs on trails in Extremadura before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access this mountain biking route.

faqs.question: Where can I find parking for this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Villanueva del Fresno, you should be able to find parking within or near the Villanueva del Fresno Town Center.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the route passes through Villanueva del Fresno Town Center and Higuera de Vargas, you can expect to find local cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in these towns.
